tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine, significant proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, glucosuria, ketonuria), suggesting acute or chronic renal dysfunction with possible nephrotic features. Low total CO₂ may indicate metabolic acidosis. HbA1c is low, arguing against chronic hyperglycemia. Urinalysis does not suggest infection. Prompt nephrology evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
